articles.title: Artemis II Mission Successfully Leaves Earth's Orbit for Lunar Journey
articles.summary: The Artemis II mission, carrying four astronauts in the Orion spacecraft, has successfully left Earth's orbit after a translunar injection engine burn lasting five minutes and 55 seconds. This critical maneuver, described as flawless by NASA's Dr. Lori Glaze, propelled the crew on a path that will take them around the far side of the Moon and back, marking the first human travel beyond Earth's orbit since 1972. Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen expressed the crew's excitement and gratitude, highlighting the collective effort behind the mission. As Earth shrinks in view, the astronauts are set to travel farther from our planet than any previous mission, potentially surpassing the record set by Apollo 13. Commander Reid Wiseman shared awe-inspiring views of Earth from space. Despite the significant engine burn, mission controllers retain the ability to return the crew to Earth if necessary, ensuring safety. This mission demonstrates advanced space exploration capabilities and international collaboration, with Hansen being the first non-American to journey to the Moon.

articles.title: Funeral Director's Misconduct Sparks Legal Campaign for Change
articles.summary: Jasmine Beverley, mother of stillborn baby Sunny Beverley-Conlin, is advocating for legal reform after discovering misconduct by funeral director Robert Bush. Sunny was stillborn in May 2022, and Bush, of Legacy Independent Funeral Directors in Hull, arranged his funeral. Bush admitted in court to 30 counts of preventing a lawful burial and one theft from charities, involving giving grieving families incorrect ashes while bodies were left unattended for months. Although Bush faced no charges related to Sunny due to legal technicalities, Jasmine became suspicious when she received Sunny's ashes in the same box she had used, noting a distinctive mark. In 2024, police informed her that Sunny's remains were likely found at Bush's site, causing her distress during a subsequent pregnancy. Bush had previously admitted to deceiving other women about ashes of their unborn babies. Jasmine emphasizes that Bush must face consequences and is pushing for law changes to protect families from similar trauma, highlighting issues of morality and accountability in funeral services.

articles.title: M&S Executive Urges Stronger Response to Rising Retail Crime
articles.summary: Marks & Spencer's retail director, Thinus Keeve, has called for increased action to combat retail crime, highlighting a surge in incidents involving shoplifting and violence against staff. He reports that retail workers face daily abuse and violence, with recent events including gangs forcing open cabinets, individuals stealing high-value items like steak, and assaults on security guards. In Clapham, south London, police responded to anti-social behavior by hundreds of young people near an M&S store. Keeve has written to London Mayor Sadiq Khan and Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood, urging enhanced police resources and a coordinated response across government, policing, and industry. He emphasizes that retail crime has become more brazen, organized, and aggressive, requiring a faster and more consistent police approach using existing tools to target repeat offenders and hotspots. M&S has invested heavily in security, but external affairs director Adam Hawksbee notes that without additional police intervention and data-driven strategies, efforts are limited. Authorities, including Crime and Policing Minister Sarah Jones, acknowledge the issue, promising stronger powers and an end to immunity for minor thefts to prosecute offenders effectively.

articles.title: Artemis II Astronauts Reflect on Space Mission to the Moon
articles.summary: The Artemis II spacecraft has successfully departed Earth's orbit and is en route to explore the far side of the Moon, carrying four astronauts who are sharing insights from their journey. Victor Glover, the pilot, emphasized the unifying view of Earth from space, stating it appears beautiful and that humanity looks like one people. This mission represents a significant step in lunar exploration, aiming to advance scientific knowledge and inspire global cooperation. The astronauts discuss their experiences and lessons learned in space, highlighting the challenges and rewards of space travel. Their reflections underscore the importance of teamwork and innovation in achieving ambitious goals like reaching the Moon. The Artemis program builds on past missions to push the boundaries of human spaceflight.

articles.title: Changes at the Justice Department After Bondi's Departure
articles.summary: President Trump has removed Attorney General Pam Bondi from her position after 14 months, marking the second Cabinet dismissal in under a month. Bondi, who led the Justice Department, faced bipartisan criticism for her management of the investigation into Jeffrey Epstein, a convicted sex offender. Additionally, the president expressed dissatisfaction with her failure to prosecute individuals he views as political opponents. Despite this, Bondi was a strong supporter of Trump, often defending him publicly, including during a contentious hearing in February. In her departure statement, Bondi described her tenure as the honor of a lifetime and claimed it was the most impactful first year in the department's history. The article explores the implications of her firing, with Ali Rogin interviewing Mary McCord to discuss potential future directions for the Justice Department, focusing on leadership transitions and policy shifts.

articles.title: Approval and Challenges for Trump's White House Ballroom Project
articles.summary: The National Capital Planning Commission, a 12-member agency overseeing federal construction in Washington, D.C., approved President Trump's White House ballroom project. Led by Trump appointees, the commission voted overwhelmingly, citing operational needs and dignified design. However, legal hurdles persist: a judge ordered a construction halt, stating Trump is a 'steward and not owner' of the White House and requiring Congressional approval. Trump disputes this necessity. In related news, Army Chief of Staff General Randy George is stepping down immediately at Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's request, part of over a dozen high-level military dismissals. He will be replaced temporarily by General Christopher LaNeve. Additionally, Democratic Party leaders, including Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer and House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, filed a lawsuit to block Trump's executive order on mail-in voting, arguing he lacks authority for such changes. The order proposes a federal list for mail-in voters and threatens fund withholding from non-compliant states. A White House spokesperson defended it as lawful election security. In Colorado, an appeals court ordered resentencing for former county clerk Tina Peters, imprisoned for election fraud efforts in 2020, ruling that her promotion of conspiracies should not have influenced her 2024 sentencing due to free speech violations.
